source: Asteria Star — Scientific Data Engine & Knowledge Graph

sources.name: NASA ADS
sources.scope: Bibliographic index of peer-reviewed astronomy and astrophysics literature.

sources.name: Arianespace
sources.scope: Ariane and Vega launch-vehicle families — user's manuals and mission data.

sources.name: Blue Origin
sources.scope: New Shepard, New Glenn, and the BE-3/BE-4 engines — manufacturer specifications.

sources.name: Britannica
sources.scope: General reference for the history of astronomy, biographies, and cultural context.

sources.name: CelesTrak
sources.scope: Two-line orbital elements (TLEs) for satellites including the ISS, used with a propagator to predict passes.

sources.name: CSA
sources.scope: Canadian missions, instruments, and contributions to international programs.

sources.name: DESI
sources.scope: Spectroscopic redshift survey measuring baryon acoustic oscillations and dark energy.

sources.name: NASA DONKI
sources.scope: The Space Weather Database Of Notifications, Knowledge, Information: solar flares, CMEs, and geomagnetic storms.

sources.name: Event Horizon Telescope
sources.scope: Horizon-scale images of supermassive black holes (M87*, Sagittarius A*).

sources.name: ESA
sources.scope: European missions, observatories, and space science imagery.

sources.name: ESA/Hubble
sources.scope: The European Hubble image archive and press releases.

sources.name: ESA/Webb
sources.scope: The European James Webb Space Telescope image archive and press releases.

sources.name: ESO
sources.scope: Southern-hemisphere observatory data and imagery (VLT, ALMA partner).

sources.name: ESA Euclid
sources.scope: Wide-field mapping of dark matter and dark energy via weak lensing and galaxy clustering.

sources.name: Gaia
sources.scope: Billion-star astrometry, photometry, and distances (future identifier support).

sources.name: Gunter's Space Page
sources.scope: A specialist reference compilation of launch vehicles, stages, and spacecraft across all spacefaring nations.

sources.name: Hipparcos
sources.scope: High-precision parallax, magnitude, and position for ~118,000 stars.

sources.name: HYG Database
sources.organization: HYG Database (Hipparcos + Yale Bright Star + Gliese), by D. Nash / astronexus
sources.scope: Aggregated, openly-licensed star catalogue combining Hipparcos, the Yale Bright Star Catalogue, and the Gliese Catalogue of Nearby Stars.

sources.name: IAU
sources.scope: Official naming, definitions, constellation boundaries, and astronomical nomenclature.

sources.name: IMO
sources.scope: Meteor shower activity, radiants, and peak forecasts.

sources.name: ISRO
sources.scope: Indian missions (Chandrayaan, Mangalyaan) and launch vehicles.

sources.name: JAXA
sources.scope: Japanese missions (Hayabusa, Akatsuki) and space science.

sources.name: NASA JPL
sources.organization: Jet Propulsion Laboratory / Solar System Dynamics
sources.scope: Orbital data, ephemerides, and small-body parameters for planets, asteroids, and comets.

sources.name: LIGO Scientific Collaboration
sources.scope: Direct detections of gravitational waves from compact-object mergers.

sources.name: Minor Planet Center
sources.scope: Designations and orbits of asteroids, comets, and minor bodies.

sources.name: NASA
sources.scope: Mission data, planetary science, space telescopes, and public-domain imagery.

sources.name: NED
sources.scope: Reference database for extragalactic objects (galaxies, quasars).

sources.name: NOAA Solar Calculator
sources.scope: Public-domain solar-position geometry: sunrise, sunset, solar noon, twilight, declination, and the equation of time.

sources.name: The Nobel Prize
sources.scope: Official record of Nobel laureates, prize motivations, and award years.

sources.name: NSF NOIRLab
sources.organization: NSF National Optical-Infrared Astronomy Research Laboratory
sources.scope: Ground-based optical/infrared observatory data and imagery.

sources.name: OpenNGC
sources.organization: OpenNGC (M. Verga) — NGC/IC, Messier, and Caldwell catalogue data
sources.scope: Openly-licensed deep-sky catalogue: object types, positions, magnitudes, sizes, morphology, and cross-identifiers.

sources.name: Planck Collaboration
sources.scope: Cosmic microwave background maps and the cosmological parameters of the ΛCDM model.

sources.name: Rocket Lab
sources.scope: Electron, Neutron, and the Rutherford/Archimedes engines — manufacturer specifications.

sources.name: Roscosmos
sources.scope: Russian crewed and robotic spaceflight history and programs.

sources.name: SDSS
sources.scope: Multi-wavelength survey of galaxies, quasars, and large-scale structure.

sources.name: SIMBAD
sources.organization: Centre de données astronomiques de Strasbourg (CDS)
sources.scope: Reference database of astronomical objects, identifiers, and measurements.

sources.name: SpaceX
sources.scope: Falcon 9, Falcon Heavy, Starship, and the Merlin/Raptor engines — manufacturer specifications and launch data.

sources.name: STScI
sources.scope: Science operations and public image archives for the Hubble and James Webb space telescopes (MAST, HubbleSite, webbtelescope.org).

sources.name: NOAA SWPC
sources.scope: Space-weather forecasts and alerts: aurora (OVATION), the Kp index, and the G1–G5 geomagnetic storm scale.

sources.name: ULA
sources.scope: Atlas V, Delta IV, and Vulcan Centaur — manufacturer specifications and mission data.

sources.name: US Naval Observatory
sources.scope: Precise time, almanac data, Sun/Moon rise-set, and phases.

sources.name: Wikidata
sources.organization: Wikidata (Wikimedia Foundation) — community-curated structured knowledge base
sources.scope: Structured mission/spacecraft facts (international designator, operator, manufacturer, launch mass); statements link to their own citations where provided.

sources.name: Wikimedia Commons
sources.scope: Openly licensed and public-domain space imagery.
